hypothesis: The previous same-concurrency diagnostic may have failed because ylong_http_client used one thread, runtime, and client per worker while libcurl used one multi handle. A single-runtime, single-public-Client ylong async concurrency model may be a fairer real-client-path comparison against libcurl multi.

source_changes.summary: Adds YLONG_BENCH_YLONG_CONCURRENCY_MODEL=threaded|single-client. Default threaded preserves existing canonical and diagnostic behavior; single-client explicitly uses one async public Client shared across in-flight ylong request workers when concurrency > 1.
Adds --ylong-concurrency-model, forwards it to the benchmark binary, records it in raw output and env JSON, and keeps it as a summary/comparison grouping dimension.
Covers explicit single-client environment wiring and verifies that comparison rows are grouped by ylong_concurrency_model so threaded and single-client results cannot be merged accidentally.

red.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_single_client_red cargo test -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench ut_ylong_concurrency_model_defaults_to_threaded_and_requires_known_value --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0 -- --exact
red.observed: failed before implementation because YlongConcurrencyModel did not exist
failed before implementation because build_benchmark_env did not accept ylong_concurrency_model

green.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_single_client_green cargo test -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench ut_ylong_concurrency_model_defaults_to_threaded_and_requires_known_value --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0
green.observed: 1 test passed; this rerun avoided the earlier --exact path mismatch that filtered all tests

verification_commands.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ylong_http_target_single_client_tests cargo test -p ylong_http_client --bin https_proxy_bench --no-default-features --features async,http1_1,ylong_base,c_openssl_3_0

verification_commands.observed: passed; stable rustfmt reported existing warnings for nightly-only rustfmt.toml options
release benchmark binary built successfully with existing repository warnings

benchmark.command: PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" python3 docs/benchmarks/run_https_proxy_bench.py --baseline libcurl --ylong-client async --ylong-concurrency-model single-client --scenario all --requests 200,1000 --repeats 3 --warmup 50 --concurrency 8 --bench-bin /tmp/ylong_http_target_single_client_release/release/https_proxy_bench --result-dir docs/benchmarks/results/async-single-client-concurrency-candidate --figure-dir docs/benchmarks/results/async-single-client-concurrency-candidate

verdict: The single-runtime, single-public-Client ylong async concurrency model is a SOTA counterexample, not a completion path. It refutes the narrower hypothesis that the previous concurrent failure was caused only by per-worker runtime/client construction. Even with one public ylong client, throughput geomean is 0.895x of libcurl, worst throughput is 0.676x, p50/p95 gates fail, CPU/request geomean is 1.588x, CPU max is 2.265x, and RSS max is 1.176x. S9 remains active and S10 must not run as a completion gate.
completion_effect: Adds causal narrowing evidence for S9 but does not complete S9 and does not permit goal_achieved.
